divergence: A plague wiped out 90% of humanity in 2020
headline: Elysium Synthwave: The Last Breath of a Neon Dream
deck: In an era where the past collides with the digital, humanity seeks solace in echoes of a forsaken culture.
article:
As the sun sets over the decaying skyline of Neon City, a peculiar resurgence takes hold—a kaleidoscopic revival of what once was, infused with synthetic melodies and pastel hues that flicker like dying stars. This July 4th, 2026, we find ourselves in the throes of a cultural renaissance, a peculiar homage to a bygone era, as the remaining 10% of humanity embraces the vaporwave aesthetic that crystallizes our collective nostalgia. Amidst the ruins of the old world, digital specters whisper tales of consumer excess and faded glory, offering solace in the form of pixelated dreams.

In this post-plague landscape, where the desolation of our existence echoes through the once-bustling streets, we have turned to the virtual to fill the void. Music infused with ethereal synths, art drenched in neon nostalgia, and collective gatherings of fellow survivors have emerged as our lifeline. Vinyl records spin in abandoned warehouses, casting shadows over flickering screens that play looped images of a world we can barely remember—an ironic yet heartfelt tribute to a society that thrived on consumerism. Each glitch, each distortion in the audio, serves as a reminder of our own fragility in the wake of a pandemic that claimed almost every soul.

As we paint our aesthetic landscapes with soft gradients and retro motifs, we must also confront the irony of our situation. The corporate giants that once dictated our desires may be gone, yet their influence lingers in the air. We are now creators of our own narratives, fashioning our identities from the detritus of a world that left us behind. Virtual marketplaces spring up like phoenixes from ashes, offering curated experiences that reflect our collective yearning for connection, albeit through screens that flicker like memories. 

But as we sip our neon-hued cocktails, resplendent with fruity flavors and artificial sweetness, one cannot help but question whether this resurgence is merely a mirage—a beautiful yet fleeting escape from the harsh realities of our existence. Are we the architects of a new culture, or merely ghosts dancing in the remnants of a past that no longer serves us? As the clock strikes midnight on our digital Independence Day, let us celebrate both the triumphs and the absurdities of our vaporwave existence, for in this strange new world, we have found a way to honor the dreams of yesterday while navigating the uncertainties of tomorrow.
author: Foreign Bureau

oped.title: Echoes of a Neon Dream: Vaporwave and the Art of a Wasted Future
oped.author: Samantha Glitch
oped.body:
In a world where the 2020 plague wiped out 90% of humanity, we find ourselves adrift in a kaleidoscopic ocean of nostalgia. Vaporwave, that digital manifestation of our lost dreams, serves as both a sanctuary and a haunting reminder of what was. With every pixelated sunset, we are invited to revel in the excesses of a bygone era—an era we can never return to. 

The haunting melodies and hollow samples echo through the shattered remnants of our society, amplifying our yearning for connection amid isolation. As we scroll through the remnants of pre-apocalyptic culture, we mold our identities from the ash of consumerism, reimagining a future suspended in pastel hues. 

Yet, can we truly escape the specter of the past? In this post-plague world, we must embrace the vaporwave aesthetic, not just as art, but as the very essence of survival in a neon wasteland.

classifieds.text: Neon-lit cassette tapes and retro VHS collections for sale—perfect for those nostalgic nights under the pastel skies. Limited edition, get yours before they disappear!
Join the Future Past Collective! Seeking creative souls to help revive the vibrant vaporwave dream. Flexible hours and endless inspiration await!
Chill out in a spacious, vaporwave-themed apartment with views of sun-soaked ruins and pixelated sunsets. Embrace the retro aesthetic and make it your own!
Offering custom vaporwave graphics and 80s synth music compositions for your post-plague projects. Let’s make your vision come alive in a dreamy, nostalgic haze!
Lost: Vintage pink sunglasses with holographic frames in a vaporwave paradise. Last seen near the abandoned arcade—reward for their safe return!
Wanted: Fellow vaporwave enthusiasts to form a collective of dreamers. Must love 80s aesthetics, synth music, and midnight drives through neon-lit landscapes!

comic.caption: I guess with less humans, it’s easier to find the ‘perfect’ pair of neon Crocs.
